LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF HEFFERNAN INSURANCE BROKERS IS OVERRULED. HEFFERNAN'S CROSS-CLAIMS ARE NOT EMBRACED OR AFFECTED BY THE APPEAL OF THE MARCH 16, 2016 ORDER DENYING AUCRA'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. HOWEVER, WHILE CCP 916 DOES NOT DEPRIVE THIS COURT OF JURISDICTION REGARDING THOSE CROSS-CLAIMS, THERE ARE SERIOUS ISSUES OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY ABOUT WHETHER A TRIAL ON THE CROSS-CLAIMS SHOULD BE DEFERRED UNTIL AFTER THE APPEAL IS RESOLVED AND THUS THE RULING ON THIS DEMURRER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS, AT THE APPROPRIATE TIME, MOVING TO STAY THE TRIAL AND/OR POSSIBLY OTHER PROCEEDINGS BASED ON JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND RELATED GROUNDS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Cross-defendants' demurrer to the cross-complaint of Heffernan Insurance Brokers is overruled. Heffernan's cross-claims are not embraced or affected by the appeal of the March 16, 2016 order denying AUCRA's motion to compel arbitration. However, while CCP 916 does not deprive this court of jurisdiction regarding those cross-claims, there are serious issues of judicial economy about whether a trial on the cross-claims should be deferred until after the appeal is resolved and thus the ruling on this demurrer is without prejudice to cross-defendants, at the appropriate time, moving to stay the trial and/or possibly other proceedings based on judicial economy and related grounds.
